title: Creamy Turkish Chicken Stir-fry
description: A festive and herbal Turkish dish featuring a creamy sauce made from chicken stock, low-sodium-friendly seasonings, and vegetables.

ingredients.name: Butter
ingredients.notes: Use at least 40% less than traditional butter for low-sodium version.

instructions: In a large saucepan, combine chicken broth and low-sodium stock. Bring to a simmer.
Add butter and minced garlic to the pan; sauté for about 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add finely chopped onion and diced celery to the saucepan, stirring occasionally for about 5 minutes or until vegetables are soft.
Stir in cumin seeds and turmeric powder. Cook for another minute.
Gradually add milk (or coconut milk), and paprika. Stir continuously over medium heat until mixture thickens slightly.
Simmer the saucepan on low for an additional 5 minutes, stirring frequently to avoid burning.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve hot over a bed of couscous or rice.
